criterion_text: Has already initiated curative treatment for the current episode of cancer

Meets either of the following: the patient has been on a stable dose of nintedanib or pirfenidone for at least 8 weeks prior to screening and during screening (nintedanib ≥100 mg BID, pirfenidone ≥400 mg TID, no dose changes), tolerates the treatment, and plans to continue this background therapy during the study; OR the patient has not received nintedanib or pirfenidone for at least 4 weeks prior to screening and during screening (previous treatment discontinued or treatment-naïve), and does not plan to initiate or re-initiate nintedanib or pirfenidone during the study.
Thyroid hormones within normal range without anti thyroid drugs
Serology compatible with primary EBV infection (anti-EBNA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgM positive OR anti-EBNA IgG negative, anti-VCA IgG positive, anti-VCA IgM positive)
No ME/CFS (ICD-10 G93.3)
Functional hyposplenism or asplenia, patient under long-term antibiotic prophylaxis for this reason
Resides in a rural county served by the specified hospital sites with Rural-Urban Continuum Code (RUCC) 4-9

At least 1 measurable lesion according to RECIST v1.1, which (in the Investigator's opinion) has had objective radiological progression on or after the last therapy, or at least one assessable lesion e.g. pleural or peritoneal thickening that does not fulfil RECIST v1.1 criteria for measurable disease.
Aplasia defined by circulating neutrophil count < 500 cells/mm³
If patient: Graves' disease with positive TSH-receptor antibodies and thyroid hormones above the upper reference limit at diagnosis
Minor or adult patient ≥ 4 years of age
Patients with uncomplicated rhegmatogenous retinal detcahment.
History of interstitial lung disease
Patient affiliated to the French Social Security system or benefiting from an equivalent plan
Adult (>18 years) males and females
Patients with known or suspected CAEBV must be 1 year of age or older
Presence of psychiatric, neurological, endocrinological and/or developmental conditions and taking medications for these conditions
Intracranial vascular malformations or dural AVF
Secondary
Subjects over the age of 90 years old
Diffusing capacity of the lung for carbon monoxide percent predicted (DLCO% predicted), corrected for hemoglobin (Hb), ≥30% and ≤90% during the screening period.
Able to understand and sign an informed consent form (ICF) that has been approved by an ethics committee.

Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including but not limited to, ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, interstitial lung disease, serious chronic gastrointestinal conditions associated with diarrhea, as determined per treating physician
Age: Children and adults from 3 to 64 years old (18 to 64 years old for controls)
The subject and his/her guardian are willing to comply with the procedures and operations specified in the study protocol
Past history of phacovitrectomy in the planned operative eye.
Inability or unwillingness to comply with repeated assessments
The subject is known to be allergic to any azole antifungal therapy or other ingredients contained in the study drug
Biliary pancreatitis diagnosed on imaging (ultrasound, magnetic resonance imaging and/or tomography)
The researcher believes that the condition of the child may interfere with study participation or other inappropriate conditions
Patients willing and able to provide simple consent for registry participation.
Recovery of pancreatitis by tolerance of oral intake (defined as 24 hours of food consumption of any consistency without emetic episodes and pain defined as 4/10 on the visual analogue score of pain)
Evidence of active or prior Crohn's disease.

Able to provide proof of active intrauterine pregnancy and estimated due date, via online document upload, using any of: ultrasound report of current pregnancy, beta hcg lab report, a signed provider's medical documentation confirming intrauterine pregnancy with fetal heart sounds and estimated due date, or a signed provider's note on a prescription sheet or physician letterhead confirming intrauterine pregnancy and estimated due date.
Pregnant.
Traumatic brain injury with unconsciousness

Known immunocompromised status including, but not limited to, individuals who have undergone organ transplantation, chemotherapy or radiotherapy within the past 12 months, who have clinically significant leukopenia, who are positive for the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) or whose immune status makes the subject a poor candidate for clinical trial participation in the opinion of the Investigator.
Patients with heart failure
Presence of organ failure

Meets the 2021 KDIGO definition of steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome (SRNS), and either: has received an adequate dose of calcineurin inhibitors (CNIs) for more than 6 months without achieving at least partial remission; or has contraindications to CNI use (e.g., eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m², prominent tubular injury on biopsy, elevated urinary markers > 3x ULN, abnormal glucose tolerance, severe uncontrolled hypertension, significant drug interactions, or known CNI allergy); or has shown inadequate response or relapse after treatment with at least two immunosuppressive agents (including a CNI and at least one other conventional or biologic agent), defined as failure to achieve complete remission after 12 months of therapy or relapse following initial response
